Cereus peruvianus is a type of cactus plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant has been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The bioactive constituents of Cereus peruvianus are believed to be responsible for its potential health benefits, including anti-diabetic, anti-cancer, and anti-aging properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Cereus peruvianus are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
